Ad Text:
This role sits within a major APS social policy program currently undergoing a large-scale, high-profile reform focused on the delivery and funding of a multi-$bn national program.
 
The successful person will join a 5-person Data Science & Analytics team within the broader data and modelling function supporting funding optimisation, classification modelling, pricing decisions, forecasting, and operational policy analytics across the program.
 
The team operates as a central data and insights capability, working closely with SES, operational, program, and policy stakeholders to support real-world funding, classification, and service delivery decisions.
 
The Opportunity

You’ll be joining a cohesive, outcomes-focused, technically advanced team with a clear pipeline of work supporting a genuine long-term reform program with significant operational and policy impact.
 
The environment is highly hands-on, with the team continuously refining and retraining production-level models using live operational data, emerging variables, assessor feedback, and evolving policy settings.

Work on complex quantitative and statistical problems through model retraining, reclassification, feature engineering, and decision-tree optimisation
Apply ML in a real-world production environment across decision trees, logistic regression, GLMs, LASSO regression, time-series modelling, with an NLP/text analytics capability currently moving into production
Fully funded EL1 equivalent contract ASAP – 30/6/27 with no incumbent, strong extension potential, and long-term reform pipeline
Advanced modelling and ML within a production R environment supporting live operational decisions
Solve complex quantitative and statistical problems through model retraining, reclassification, feature engineering, and decision-tree optimisation
Long-term funding stability, clear project pipeline, smart technical team, and meaningful work with real-world policy impact
The Role

As part of the Data Science & Analytics team, you’ll work across the full analytics lifecycle from sourcing and transforming raw data through to refining production models and delivering policy-relevant insights.
 
You’ll be joining a mature R Shop (science/ engineering environment team  with models in in production not just on desktops). The team deliberately uses R-centric pipelines over SQL due to the complexity of the multi-table data structures they work with. Once extracted, transformation logic, feature engineering, model optimisation, and analytical iteration are significantly more effective and maintainable within the R ecosystem.
 
Key responsibilities include:

Extracting, joining, transforming, and engineering large Oracle-based datasets using R pipelines and ETL workflows
Building, refining, retraining, and optimising statistical and machine learning models operating in live production environments
Analysing cohort behaviour, utilisation trends, funding distributions, and emerging variables impacting classification and model performance
Working closely with SES, policy, program, and operational stakeholders to provide data-driven insights supporting funding, classification, and operational policy decisions
The Ideal Person

You’re a Data Scientist, Quantitative Analyst, or Analytics professional who enjoys solving complex quantitative problems while also engaging with program, operational, and policy stakeholders to support real-world decision making.
 
The successful candidate will bring a combination across:

Advanced R (data science and engineering into models)
Strong quantitative and statistical modelling capability across techniques such as logistic regression, decision trees, GLMs, LASSO regression, time-series analysis, or related ML approaches
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability and genuine desire to work closely with SES, policy, and program teams to understand business problems and provide meaningful data-driven insights
A collaborative and pragmatic mindset, with the ability to translate technical outputs into practical operational and policy context within an evolving reform environment where models, business rules, and policy settings continue to mature
Due to the nature of the work and datasets, Australian Citizenship with Baseline or above and current residency in Canberra are mandatory requirements.
 
Salary / Rate
This role is being offered as a full-time EL1 equivalent rate contract
ASAP – 30/6/27 with likely extension options
How to Apply

Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Lucas Walker on 02 0483 911 561 quoting ref no. 21590

Identify trends, risks, inefficiencies and opportunities at store, region, state and national level

Build and maintain commercial performance dashboards and reporting frameworks

Develop and track cost metrics, margin drivers, wage efficiency and productivity measures

Support pricing, promotional and commercial decisions with data-driven insight

Provide clear, practical recommendations that State and Regional teams can action

AI & Automation Integration

Identify opportunities to deploy AI tools and automation across:

Commercial reporting

Operational workflows

Forecasting and scenario modelling

Labour and productivity analysis

Work with internal stakeholders to integrate AI into day-to-day decision-making, not just theory

Improve speed, accuracy and consistency of reporting across the network

Act as a bridge between commercial insight, operations and emerging technology

Operational & Cross-Functional Support

Partner with teams across:

Finance

Operations

Property & network development

Marketing

WH&S and compliance

Analyse operational performance and link it back to financial outcomes

Support business cases for new initiatives, investments and network changes

Review store-level execution against strategy and identify improvement opportunities

Stakeholder Engagement & Governance

Present insights and recommendations to senior leadership and state teams

Support Regional and State Managers with commercial insight to drive accountability

Translate complex data into clear, simple language for non-financial stakeholders

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:

Experience & Capability

Proven experience in a commercial analyst or business operations analyst role

Strong capability in analysing P&Ls, balance sheets and multi-site retail or franchise networks

Experience working with large operational datasets and turning them into insight

Retail, franchise, multi-site or operationally complex business experience highly regarded

Technical Skills

Advanced Excel / data modelling skills

Strong reporting and dashboarding capability (Power BI, Tableau or similar preferred)

Commercial acumen — understands how decisions impact profitability and cash

Ability to build metrics that actually drive behaviour, not just reporting

Personal Attributes

Commercially curious — asks why, not just what

Confident communicator who can challenge constructively

Comfortable working with senior leaders and frontline teams alike

Highly organised, self-driven and outcomes-focused

Adaptable and comfortable working across diverse parts of the business




WHY JOIN BOB JANE T-MARTS:

Work directly with the COO and senior leadership

Influence decisions across a national retail and franchise network

Play a key role in shaping how AI and data are used in a large Australian business

Broad exposure across finance, operations, property, marketing and safety

A role with real authority, visibility and impact — not just reporting for reporting’s sake

Working within a layered modelling approach (Bronze → Silver Standardised → Silver Canonical → Gold → Data Product)

Partnering with cross‑functional teams to deliver analytics‑ready data assets aligned to financial services needs

Very strong experience designing conceptual, logical and physical data models in financial services environments

Desirable experience:

Familiarity with lakehouse architectures such as Databricks or similar platforms
Exposure to data governance, metadata management and alignment with Data Stewards
Experience building reusable domain models that support financial analytics, reporting and downstream data products

About the Australian Psychological Society (APS)

The Australian Psychological Society (APS) is the foremost professional body and voice for psychological science and practice in Australia. From professional development and support services to advocating in government, we cater to the needs of members and help grow the capacity of the psychology workforce. While in the general public we provide the psychology information and tools people need, ultimately we serve our purpose of improving the mental health and wellbeing of the Australian population.

Stronger Together, Respect in every action, Forward Thinking, Professional Integrity




About the Role

We are passionate about our people and it is with pleasure that we announce an exciting opportunity - we are recruiting for a Senior Policy Advisor to join the APS Policy and Research team in a part time FTE0.6 (3 days/week) capacity.

The Senior Policy Adviser provides the research, analysis and material that forms the policy direction for APS' strategic priorities. The position works on plans and programs for policy research and analysis of issues and trends that affect the work of APS members and community wellbeing more broadly.

The Senior Policy Advisor primary responsibilities include:

Policy development - assist to develop policies, undertake program of policy briefs, submissions, review papers and position statements, evaluation and report on trends and issues, collaborate with relevant teams and synthesise diverse opinions of members and other stakeholders to develop evidence-informed positions.

Reporting and communicating - present policy materials internally and externally, represent the APS in policy and health forums, provide policy advice and regularly inform members of policy and advocacy initiatives.

Research and analysis - undertake research and analysis to develop policy plans and programs, critically analyse literature to develop options and utilise research, government reports and member input to respond to issues.

Member support and engagement - Consult and collaborate with members to utilise their expertise and inform policy, maintain relationships with Member Groups and support the Professional Resources team to identify areas of need.

External representation and collaboration - represent the APS to develop and maintain networks, work collaboratively with government departments and other stakeholders on issues and projects relevant to psychology.




We have staff in most states and territories, and the role is open to applicants located anywhere within Australia.

We work in a remote-hybrid model depending on where you are based. Most team members currently work from home, with the option to work in our Melbourne CBD office when it suits them, or as the business requires (ie for large planning meetings).




Our Ideal Candidate

We are looking for a motivated and experienced individual with exceptional communication skills, a broad understanding of public policy issues and deep knowledge of the discipline and practice of psychology.

Key Selection criteria includes:

Relevant tertiary qualifications in psychology and current Ahpra registration.

A contemporary knowledge of the discipline and practice of psychology.

Demonstrated experience in policy roles, ideally in relation to professional services such as mental health or health.

An ability to interpret complex business or government policies and explain them to others.

Strong experience in analysing data, understanding trends and interpreting statistics.

Highly developed written and oral presentation skills, with ability to identify key messages and information required for decision making. Able to effectively represent the organisation, collaborate effectively with others and coherently present ideas.

Ability to manage high workloads and effectively prioritise in times of conflicting deadlines.




Why work with the APS?

In addition to meaningful work and a supportive team, some of the great employee benefits offered at the APS include:

Access to salary packaging benefits including Salary packaging Living allowance $15,900 (net) & Meal Entertainment Benefit $2,650 (net) putting more money in your pocket.

Flexible approach to work with no mandated days in the office to provide better work/life balance

Great social environment to keep us connected

12 weeks paid parental leave

3 paid volunteer days each year

Employee Assistance Program & Mental Health First Aiders

Staff access to Member Benefits Program

Paid Employee Referral Program, where you are rewarded for referring successful talent




Apply today

If this role sounds like your next career step then please submit your application addressing the key selection criteria in a cover letter, along with your current CV.

Applications will close COB on Monday 1st June.

DWS is seeking a Data Analyst to join a large‑scale financial services Data Platform Modernisation Program, supporting the transition to a modern, enterprise‑grade data platform built on Databricks. Working closely with Business Analysts, Data Modellers, Data Stewards and business stakeholders, this role plays a critical part in delivering trusted, high‑quality data that supports regulatory reporting, risk, analytics and decision‑making within a complex financial services environment.
The Data Analyst will analyse, profile and validate financial data as it moves from source systems through standardisation and governance to analysis‑ready datasets. Financial services domain experience is highly desirable, with a strong understanding of the importance of data quality, lineage, controls and consistency in regulated environments.
 

Where: Melbourne CBD
Type: Max Term
Start: ASAP
Length: 12 months, extention possible
Style: Hybrid; 3+ days onsite
Salary: Up to $185K (inc. super) depending on experience

 

Key responsibilities include:
Defining cleaned and conformed entity structures across financial services source systems
Mapping source financial data types to standardised logical data models
Defining deduplication logic and uniqueness rules for customer, account and transactional data
Defining SCD strategies (Type 1, 2 or 3) aligned to financial and regulatory business requirements
Defining and documenting robust data quality rules including nullability, ranges, referential integrity and format validation
Mapping source fields to standardised naming conventions aligned to enterprise financial data standards
Producing logical ERDs across data layers, including attributes, relationships and cardinality
Creating and maintaining attribute catalogues, SCD strategy documents and data quality rule catalogues
Developing detailed source‑to‑target mapping documentation across Bronze, Silver (standardised and canonical) and Gold layers
To be successful in this role, you will bring:
Strong experience in data analysis, profiling and validation across complex financial services data pipelines
Proven understanding of data modelling and data engineering concepts, including entity design, SCD strategies and source‑to‑target mappings
Hands‑on experience defining and implementing data quality rules in regulated or risk‑aware environments
Significant experience working with financial data domains such as customers, products, transactions or reporting
Experience working on modern data platforms or transformation programs, ideally Databricks or lakehouse environments
Familiarity with end‑to‑end data lifecycle artefacts including ERDs, attribute catalogues and data quality frameworks
Experience working in cross‑functional, governed data teams alongside Business Analysts, Data Modellers and Data Stewards

Experience providing technical guidance for complex data and/or integration solutions in multi-stakeholder environments.

Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to engage senior stakeholders and present effectively to audiences with varying levels of analytical understanding.

You have a track record of at least 8 years delivering successful products to market.

Professional Skills

You're analytical; you know that your product must provide tangible business value and can gather the right requirements and market data analysis to support your work.

You have a bias for action, and can break down complex problems into tangible steps.
You enjoy motivating committed, smart people to do the best work of their careers.
Other things to know

Learning & Development


There is no one-size-fits-all career path at Thoughtworks: however you want to develop your career is entirely up to you. But we also balance autonomy with the strength of our cultivation culture. This means your career is supported by interactive tools, numerous development programs and teammates who want to help you grow. We see value in helping each other be our best and that extends to empowering our employees in their career journeys.

Responsible Use of AI in Recruitment


At Thoughtworks, we use AI tools to support our recruitment team with administrative tasks such as drafting communications, scheduling interviews and writing job descriptions.

Crucially, our AI tools do not screen, assess, rank or make hiring decisions. Every application is reviewed by our team and all selection decisions are made exclusively by our interviewers and hiring managers.

We are committed to fairness and responsible AI. We actively manage our AI systems by testing, monitoring for biased outcomes and implementing mitigation measures. We hold our third-party vendors to these same high standards through a rigorous governance process. For additional information, please see our full Thoughtworks AI Policy for Recruitment.

Responsible Use of AI in Recruitment


At Thoughtworks, we use AI tools to support our recruitment team with administrative tasks such as drafting communications, scheduling interviews and writing job descriptions.

Crucially, our AI tools do not screen, assess, rank or make hiring decisions. Every application is reviewed by our team and all selection decisions are made exclusively by our interviewers and hiring managers.

We are committed to fairness and responsible AI. We actively manage our AI systems by testing, monitoring for biased outcomes and implementing mitigation measures. We hold our third-party vendors to these same high standards through a rigorous governance process. For additional information, please see our full Thoughtworks AI Policy for Recruitment.

Silverchain is commencing an enterprise program to modernise our digital foundations and improve the experience for our clients, carers, providers and staff. Powered by Microsoft Dynamics 365, this multi-year transformation will bring a single, integrated platform to support care delivery at scale - advancing our role as a leading not for profit provider of home and community health services.

As an UX & Service Design Analyst, you'll help shape the end‑to‑end services and experiences that underpin this transformation. You'll work at the intersection of user needs, business priorities and technical delivery - translating insights into practical service designs blueprints that can be delivered at scale.

This is a hands‑on role where you'll influence how people experience care and how users interact with systems - ensuring designs are user‑centred, feasible and aligned to the target state of the program.

What's on offer

Fulltime two-year Fixed Term role. Monday-Friday 76 hours per fortnight.
Hybrid role - working from home flexible location VIC, WA, SA or NSW.
Working for a non-profit organisation, you can salary package up to $18,500 tax free.
Everyday discounts, including discounted vouchers for Coles, Woolworths, Amazon, Myer, The Good Guys and more.
Comprehensive health and wellbeing resources and support.

What we're looking for

To thrive in this role, you'll need to have:

Demonstrated experience in UX and service design roles within complex digital or enterprise environments.
Strong capability in service design, including customer journey mapping and service blueprinting.
Experience producing low‑ to high‑fidelity UI designs, wireframes and prototypes for mobile and responsive web experiences.
Proven facilitation skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders, explain design rationale and influence outcomes.
Experience collaborating closely with delivery teams to translate designs into implementable requirements and acceptance criteria.
Working knowledge of user‑centred design, usability and accessibility standards.
Proficiency with relevant tooling such as Figma, Miro or equivalent.

Experience designing multi‑channel services, working in health, aged care or regulated environments, or delivering solutions on enterprise platforms such as Microsoft Dynamics 365 is highly regarded but not essential.

The role

As a UX Service Design Analyst:

You are a skilled UX and service design professional who thrives in complex environments and is comfortable balancing user needs with operational, technical and regulatory constraints.

Facilitate workshops, interviews and observations with stakeholders, end users, advisory groups and delivery teams to understand user, business and service needs.
Design end‑to‑end target‑state customer and staff journeys, including personas, scenarios, pain points and success measures.
Develop service blueprints that articulate front‑stage and back‑stage interactions across channels such as mobile app, customer portal, and Microsoft Dynamics 365.
Identify service gaps, failure points, dependencies and hand‑offs across channels and teams, and document implications and recommendations.
Create low‑ to high‑fidelity UI designs, wireframes and mock‑ups for agreed components across mobile, web and enterprise platforms.
Facilitate design validation and usability sessions, synthesising feedback and iterating designs in line with user insights and delivery constraints.
Work closely with Business Analysts, Product Owners, Solution Architects, vendors and delivery leads to translate designs into clear, implementable requirements.
Maintain high‑quality design artefacts and documentation within agreed program tools and repositories.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of UX and service design practices across the Digital Program.

Why choose Silverchain

Everyone at Silverchain, from our care teams to leadership, plays a vital role in delivering Best Care. It's a team effort. Best Care is care that is safe, personal, connected and effective. This means the quality of care we provide is expected to meet the same standards as a hospital or residential aged care facility. ·         Design and recommend solutions to resolve organisational and operational problems, including CRM configuration, pipeline architecture, lifecycle stage design, and workflow automation.

·         Oversee the implementation of approved recommendations, including issuing revised procedures, configuration changes, and supporting documentation across teams.

·         Review operational procedures and reporting outputs, advising leadership on departures from agreed standards and recommending corrective actions.

·         Build and maintain real-time performance reporting frameworks, funnel metrics, and management dashboards to support data-driven decision-making.

·         Deliver structured change management activities, including staff training, adoption monitoring, and ongoing system governance to embed process improvements.

·         Evaluate emerging technologies and system capabilities for commercial relevance and present assessed recommendations to leadership with supporting business cases.

·         Scale and adapt approved organisational frameworks across multiple business units, ensuring consistency while accommodating operational differences.

·         Conduct organisational studies and reviews to improve management methods and structures.

·         Prepare recommendations for senior management regarding changes to business processes and organisational procedures.

·         Advise management on policies and strategies to improve efficiency and profitability.




Skills and Experience Required

Experience

·         Proven experience analysing and redesigning business processes, operational workflows, and organisational systems in a commercial environment.

·         Demonstrated experience developing and implementing solutions to improve sales performance, process efficiency, and forecast accuracy.

·         Experience consulting with and advising senior stakeholders, including executives, sales leadership, and operational teams.

·         Track record of delivering change management, system adoption, and staff training initiatives.

·         Experience producing detailed process documentation, workflow analysis, SOPs, and governance frameworks.

·         Experience working across multi-brand or multi-unit business structures (preferred).




Skills and Capabilities

·         Strong analytical and problem-solving capability, with the ability to evaluate complex systems and develop practical, commercially-grounded solutions.

·         Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills across executive, operational, and frontline levels.

·         Ability to translate business requirements into system configurations, process designs, and governance frameworks.

·         Strong reporting and data analysis skills, including performance dashboards, funnel metrics, and management reporting.

·         Structured approach to documentation, process design, and change management.

·         Self-directed, with the ability to lead projects and drive outcomes independently.




Qualifications

·         Bachelor's degree or higher in Business, Commerce, Management, Information Systems, or a related discipline

·         Candidates with at least five years of relevant experience in lieu of formal qualifications will be considered.

·         Relevant professional certifications or postgraduate qualifications are highly regarded.

Role Overview

Position: Senior Consultant - Transformation

Location: Melbourne (Hybrid: 3 days on-site)

Initial Duration: 1st week of June - 31st July, with a view to move to longer term contract for the delivery phase

The Opportunity

We are seeking a heavyweight, highly autonomous Senior Consultant - Transformation to spearhead the critical review and bottom-up re-set of a large-scale enterprise transformation program.

This is a two-phased engagement. Initially, you will operate in a strategic advisory capacity, conducting a rapid diagnostic of the current state, defining priorities, and designing the future roadmap. For the right candidate, this will transition into a long-term execution role, where you will own and deliver the very initiatives you have shaped.

Key Responsibilities

In this role, you will be entirely accountable for shaping the design and structure of the program. Your initial focus will center on:

Program Diagnostic & Review: Assess the current state of the large-scale transformation program. Identify bottlenecks, evaluate where the program is lagging, and determine immediate priorities.

Strategic Re-set & Design: Re-architect the program from the bottom-up. Establish robust frameworks, optimize mobilization strategies, and redefine ways of working and technology alignment.

Stakeholder Engagement & Elicitation: Partner closely with executive-level stakeholders to elicit complex business requirements, manage expectations, and align on desired outcomes.

Roadmapping & Prioritization: Restructure the program portfolio. Facilitate rigorous prioritization frameworks to determine what the new initiatives need to be, when they should launch, and what solutions must be delivered.

Recommendations: Present clear, data-driven recommendations and a finalized strategic roadmap to the executive steering committee.

About You

We need a "shaper" who can operate with a high degree of ambiguity and take total ownership.

Seniority & Autonomy: Proven track record operating at a Senior Consultant or Advisory level, ideally from a Consulting background. You are comfortable working completely independently.

Transformation Expertise: Deep experience reviewing, rescuing, or resetting troubled, large-scale enterprise transformation programs (incorporating people, process, and technology).

Executive Presence: Exceptional stakeholder management skills with a demonstrated ability to influence, challenge, and advise C-suite and executive leaders.

Strategic Architecture: Strong capability in designing program frameworks, operating models, and roadmaps from scratch.

Delivery Acumen: While the start is strategic, you possess the delivery grit required to transition into the execution phase later this year.

Domain Knowledge: Experience working in the Consulting and Higher Education space is required to successfully deliver this role
Why Apply?

High Impact: You will have the mandate to completely reshape a major program from the ground up.

Longevity: A seamless bridge from a short-term advisory piece into a multi-month/year delivery pipeline.

What to expect when you're a part of the NTI family:

About this role
As the Senior Detection & Response Analyst, you will protect nib by delivering high-quality threat intelligence and detection & response capability. You will achieve this by monitoring and analysing the evolving threat landscape, producing timely stakeholder reporting, and translating intelligence into improved detections, investigations, and threat-informed security recommendations.

Experience with log aggregation and analysis, extracting insights from large datasets (desired)
Experience in scanning tools and interpreting vulnerability reports (desired)
Specific experience configuring and managing cloud security controls (DLP, CASB, etc.) (desired)

Introduction

As a member the Promontory Australia team at IBM you will contribute to the firm's efforts in working with clients on challenging strategic, risk management, and regulatory compliance issues.

Our team of specialists offers the following services across Prudential, Conduct and AML Regulation:

· Risk Management – Providing advisory and assurance services regarding organisational transformation across risk management, governance, culture, accountability and compliance.

· Remediation Services – Assurance to regulators and firms over customer focused remediation programs.

· Regulatory Framework – Advising regulators and industry associations on building regulatory frameworks.

· Regulatory Liaison – Helping our clients navigate relationships with regulators.

· RegTech – Working with IBM teams and leveraging their technology assets in advising clients on AI based solutions and associated risk governance.

We have a diverse client base in Australia, which includes some of Australia's largest financial institutions and clients drawn from the public sector.

As a Principal, you will be responsible for managing risk, regulatory and compliance engagements on a day-to-day basis, analysing complex problems, developing recommendations to address client issues and heavily involved in reports and other deliverables. A strong consulting background with experience in non-financial risk management (including operational risk and resilience) and or superannuation is preferable. You will also be expected to draw upon your insights and networks to identify business opportunities for IBM Promontory to pursue.




Roles and Responsibilities

To be successful in this role a candidate must possess strong leadership skills and have the stature to engage effectively with clients and potential clients at all levels. The candidate will also need to inspire and work effectively with team members drawn from a range of technical disciplines. Interact comfortably with executive management across industry and regulatory authorities. Key responsibilities will include:

Manage projects and act as client point of contact on a day-to-day basis contributing to engagements being delivered to budget and meeting profit and realisation targets.

Undertake analysis as directed and take responsibility for the preparation of project deliverables, including reports and presentations.

Interact comfortably with executive management of and regulatory authorities, generally with a Managing Director present.

Strong analytical skills, oral skills, with demonstrated ability to communicate clearly, simply and logically.

Strong written communication skills with, the ability to construct well-founded, clear, and concise analyses and recommendations.

Ability to handle complex information, solve problems, and manage multiple tasks.

Ability to work closely with, and communicate effectively with, clients and within all levels of the organisation.

Strong commitment to integrity and ethics.

The candidate will also be required to build and retain relationships across the industry and to identify opportunities for further work. This includes the ability to establish and maintain positive relationships and build teams that are motivated to succeed.




Required Professional and Technical Expertise

· An undergraduate degree in either Accounting, Commerce, Finance, or Law - with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· At least 6 years experience in Financial services, Consulting, Regulator, Compliance, Risk management, Governance or legal.

· Track record working in a fast-paced and unstructured environment with aptitude for identifying and developing innovative solutions to client challenges.

· Analytics and data: Proven track record of successfully delivering highly analytical and data intensive work. Strong understanding of data governance and experience in working with large and unstructured datasets to draw meaningful and actionable insights and outcomes.

· AI: A strong understanding of AI fundamentals, including AI governance, and model development and application.

· Expert level capabilities in Excel (incl. advanced excel functionality and VBA / Macro development). Expert level capabilities in working with large datasets in SQL.




Preferred Professional and Technical Expertise

· Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance, Law or Business Administration with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.

· Specialising in data analytics, risk management, assurance, and regulatory compliance.

· Experience developing, testing and validating the end-to-end modelling process and knowledge of a variety of tools including Alteryx, R and Python.




About the Business Unit:

IBM Promontory, a business unit of IBM Consulting, is the premier consulting firm for financial services companies. IBM Promontory excels at helping clients resolve critical issues, particularly those with a regulatory dimension. Promontory professionals have unparalleled regulatory experience and insight, and provide our clients with frank, proactive advice informed by best practices and regulatory expectations.

Promontory Australia is a boutique operation and maintains links to the US and UK. Our consultants in Australia have diverse backgrounds from regulatory authorities, industry and consulting.

In recent years Promontory has completed a number of high profile engagements with Australian financial institutions and due to a strong pipeline of work we are looking to expand our team with a highly skilled, adaptable and dependable individual to join the high performing Sydney office.

Introduction

A career in IBM Consulting is built on long-term client relationships and close collaboration worldwide. You’ll work with leading companies across industries, helping them shape their hybrid cloud and AI journeys. With support from our strategic partners, robust IBM technology, and Red Hat, you’ll have the tools to drive meaningful change and accelerate client impact. At IBM Consulting, curiosity fuels success. You’ll be encouraged to challenge the norm, explore new ideas, and create innovative solutions that deliver real results. Our culture of growth and empathy focuses on your long-term career development while valuing your unique skills and experiences.

Your role and responsibilities

As a Package Consultant at IBM, you will design, develop, and re-engineer complex application components and integrate software packages to drive mission-critical client results. You will apply a blend of consultative skills and technical expertise to facilitate the hybrid cloud and AI journey for global organizations.

A SAP Data Migration Consultant plays a vital role in ensuring data integrity and consistency during the implementation of SAP projects by managing the end-to-end migration lifecycle.

Required education

Bachelor's Degree

Required technical and professional expertise

1. Strategy & Project Planning

Develop and execute comprehensive data migration strategies for SAP Hybrid (Selective Data Transition, Bluefield, Rapid Move) or traditional Brownfield/Greenfield projects.

Define scope, objectives, and deliverables while identifying and mitigating risks to ensure projects stay within timeline and budget.

Implement "early warning systems" to proactively address potential delays in the migration timeline.

2. Data Assessment & Quality Management

Assess the readiness and quality of legacy data, defining robust rules for cleansing, enrichment, and validation.

Oversee data mapping and transformation logic specifically for EAM data structures.

Implement data modeling and quality management techniques to ensure accurate data reflects in the target system.

3. Technical Execution & ETL

Design, develop, and execute migration programs and scripts using tools like SNP CrystalBridge or SAP standard migration tools.

Coordinate the end-to-end Extraction, Transformation, and Loading (ETL) process from legacy systems to SAP.

Troubleshoot technical migration activities promptly to resolve data-related issues.

4. Testing & Validation

Design and execute robust test plans, including unit, integration, and User Acceptance Tests (UAT), to guarantee functionality after migration.

Validate migrated data against business and regulatory requirements to ensure absolute data integrity.

5. Stakeholder Collaboration & Mentorship

Partner with business stakeholders to understand data requirements and validation criteria.

Collaborate with global experts, including the off shore centre and regional project teams, to maintain alignment on delivery.

Provide guidance and mentorship to team members and analysts to foster an efficient working environment.

6. Documentation, Training & Compliance

Maintain comprehensive documentation of migration processes, mapping rules, and validation criteria.

Provide training and support to business users on migration tools and offer post-migration support.

Ensure all activities comply with data security measures and organizational policies to protect sensitive information.

7. Continous Improvement:

Identify improvement opportunities for improving data migration processes and methodologies

Stay updated with the latest SAP data migration tools and technologies

Required Professional and Technical Expertise

Experience: Minimum 8 years of experience in SAP Data Migration projects (Greenfield, Brownfield, or Hybrid/Selective Data Transition).

Functional Expertise: Strong understanding of SAP data structures and business processes.

Platform Knowledge: Proven expertise in SAP S/4HANA migration using SNP tools (such as CrystalBridge) or standard SAP tools.

Soft Skills: Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills with the ability to work in fast-paced, agile environments.

Location: Sydney or Melbourne

Nine is Australia's largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you'll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country's most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands.

Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines and models that extract, load, and transform data from various sources into the data warehouse.

Champion engineering best practices across coding, DevOps, and metadata standardisations to ensure high-quality output within project timelines.
Integrate emerging technologies and AI tools into daily workflows for code suggestions, data profiling, and automated testing.
Collaborate with internal stakeholders and adjacent technology teams, including Cyber Security and Platform Engineering, to maintain organisational standards and resolve incidents.
Work closely with Product Managers to shape data solutions, understand end-user requirements, and support stakeholders through knowledge sharing.

Qualifications

A bachelor's or master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field is highly desirable.
Deep understanding of cloud migration strategies, best practices, and potential pitfalls.

What you'll bring:

Technical Mastery: Fluency in Python and SQL, with a deep understanding of OLTP and OLAP schema design concepts.
Pipeline Expertise: Proven experience leading the design of data pipelines and managing infrastructure using tools like Apache Airflow and DBT.
Cloud & DevOps Skills: Proficiency with major cloud platforms (GCP, AWS, or Azure) and CI/CD solutions like Cloud Build or Concourse.
Modern Engineering Standards: Knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform and the ability to leverage AI to support engineering tasks.
Leadership & Communication: Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to lead technical discussions, build consensus, and work effectively with cross-functional teams.
Problem-Solving Mindset: A strong analytical approach and a passion for finding creative solutions to complex technical challenges.

About UNSW

At UNSW, we pride ourselves on being a workplace where the best people come to do their best work.  We aspire to be Australia’s global university, improving and transforming lives through excellence in research, outstanding education and a commitment to advancing a just society. 

UNSW’s Lifelong Learning Unit (LLL) is a newly created business unit designed to help working professionals, alumni, and organisations upskill and reskill throughout their careers. LLL offers flexible and practical programs aligned with rapidly evolving workforce needs. Led by the inaugural Dean of Lifelong Learning, Professor Nick Wailes, the unit is central to UNSW’s strategy of enabling lifelong employability, driving inclusive access, and strengthening long‑term engagement with graduates and industry partners.

Why your role matters

Reporting to the Director, Learning Experience, the Senior Manager, Learning Design leads a high-performing team to develop scalable, high-quality online portfolios. This outcomes-focused leader will ensure strict adherence to processes and deadlines while embedding AI-enabled approaches to enhance learning, streamline development, and uplift team capability.

The successful candidate must have a deep understanding of adult learning principles and a proven track record of managing teams to deliver exceptional results. Exceptional stakeholder collaboration skills are essential to align LLL offerings with strategic goals and ensure a best-in-class learner experience.

About the Role

Accountabilities for this role include:

Lead a high-performing learning design team, fostering a culture of innovation and collaboration while building team capability and fluency in safe, ethical, and effective AI practices.
Manage resource allocation and outcomes for development squads, contractors, and subject matter experts to design and deliver high-quality, scalable online lifelong learning programs.
Identify and implement opportunities for innovation, including the evaluation, scaling, and proactive adoption of AI-enabled tools to optimize and streamline learning design workflows.
Manage end-to-end course design processes, documentation, and quality assurance, utilizing data-driven insights and AI analytics to ensure academic integrity and a best-in-class learner experience.
Collaborate closely with project managers, product teams, and the Quality Coordinator to meet strict criteria across project quality, budgets, timelines, and technology requirements.
Build strong institutional partnerships to align educational development with UNSW strategy, supporting the Director in driving the evolution of learning design through horizon scanning of emerging edtech.

About You

Relevant tertiary qualification combined with extensive experience in learning design, educational development, and a strong portfolio of successful online programs within higher education.
Proven track record of managing and enhancing the capability of high-performing teams, with an emphasis on building group fluency in AI tools for content development and operational efficiency.
Strong consultation, negotiation, and influencing skills to build productive institutional networks and manage diverse stakeholder expectations effectively.
Demonstrated project management expertise, utilizing structured methodologies to manage project lifecycles, resource allocation, and strict delivery timelines in uncertain environments.
Deep understanding of education quality assurance frameworks and international trends in online learning, particularly the integration of emerging educational technologies.
Advanced written and verbal communication skills, with a self-motivated approach to using independent judgment, solving complex problems, and proactively applying AI to improve outcomes.

For further information on the role & responsibilities, please refer to the Position Description

How to Apply: Applicants are asked to provide a CV and cover letter addressing the pertinent points of the Position Description. The application process will close on Sunday 31 May at 11:55pm.

Please note: Sponsorship is not available for this role, valid Australian working rights are required on application.

Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)

Job Title: Under Banking and Financial Services -- Funds Management
Ad Text:
Listed Company Intelligence Lead
Melbourne | Senior investment-facing role
  
Some people read company announcements.
Others understand what they really mean.

We are looking for a senior Australian equities professional with approximately 10+ years’ experience in direct equities, listed company analysis, fundamental research and valuation.

This role is for someone who can analyse Australian listed companies, interpret market developments and turn investment thinking into clear, practical insight for a senior internal audience.

This is not a sell-side publishing role, a traditional advice role or a narrow reporting seat. It is for someone who enjoys companies, markets, judgement and decision-making.
  
The work

You will:

Analyse Australian listed companies and sectors.
Review results, announcements, market updates and broker research.
Build financial models and valuation work.
Form views on quality, valuation, risk and opportunity.
Write concise investment notes and market commentary.
Contribute to senior investment discussions.
Help improve how ideas are assessed, monitored and reviewed.
What we need

Approximately 10+ years’ experience in Australian equities, funds management, equities research, stockbroking, asset consulting or another listed markets environment.
Strong direct equities experience, with depth in Australian listed companies and sectors.
Deep fundamental research, valuation and financial modelling capability.
Ability to form clear views on company quality, valuation, risk and opportunity.
Clear written and verbal communication, including the ability to translate complex investment thinking for a senior internal audience.
Commercial judgement, investment discipline and confidence working with senior stakeholders.
This role would suit someone from funds management, equities research, stockbroking, investment consulting, asset consulting or another listed markets environment.

Location: Melbourne
Role type: Full-time, permanent
Remuneration: Competitive package based on experience.

About the Role

 As a Data Scientist, you will play a pivotal role in identifying opportunities to leverage data science, machine learning, and GenAI to solve complex business challenges. You will work collaboratively with cross-functional teams to develop, deploy, and monitor advanced data-driven solutions that enhance decision-making and deliver measurable outcomes. This role will also involve close collaboration with, and will report to Gallagher Bassett’s global data science team, ensuring alignment with global strategies and sharing best practices across regions.


Key Responsibilities:

Identify opportunities for data science, machine learning, and GenAI solutions to address business challenges and deliver value at scale.

Develop and implement advanced data science and machine learning models, ensuring alignment with business objectives.

Research and evaluate competing solutions to determine optimal approaches for problem-solving.

Extract, clean, and prepare data for analysis, addressing data quality issues and performing feature engineering.

Collaborate with product managers and team members to design and deploy data science solutions.

Monitor and optimise model performance over time to ensure sustained predictive accuracy.

Develop and evaluate GenAI applications using tools such as LlamaIndex, LangChain, and LLMOps tools like DeepEval.

Create and refine prompts for querying large language models (LLMs) and assess their outputs.

Work with technology partners to build and maintain systems for deploying models and algorithms.

Provide guidance and mentorship to team members, fostering a collaborative and innovative environment.


About You

We are looking for a passionate and experienced Data Scientist who thrives in a collaborative environment and is eager to push the boundaries of innovation.

Essential Skills and Experience:

A degree in Data Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, or a related field (Master’s degree preferred).

Strong understanding of data science and machine learning concepts, methodologies, and evaluation techniques, and working experience within a data science focussed role

Minimum of 5 years of hands-on experience in end-to-end data science projects.

Proficiency in Python and its data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, Scikit-Learn, Catboost, Keras, TensorFlow, PyTorch, NumPy, Matplotlib).

Expertise in SQL for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis.

Proven ability to lead and deliver projects independently, with a track record of mentoring and guiding team members.

Experience working in cloud-based environments, preferably Azure Databricks.

Familiarity with developing business applications using GenAI/LLM technologies and building LLM workflows on platforms like Azure Foundry.

Proficiency in tools such as LlamaIndex, LangChain, and LangGraph.

Strong communication skills to effectively convey complex data science concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

Experience in insurance or claims management is highly desirable.




Why Gallagher Bassett?

At Gallagher Bassett, we pride ourselves on fostering a supportive and inclusive workplace culture.

We offer:

Attractive remuneration packaging & flexible work arrangements including flexible hybrid/Work from Home options

Opportunities for career growth and professional development

A collaborative environment where your contributions are valued

Paid Parental Leave, Reproductive leave, birthday leave and purchased lifestyle leave packages

Collaborative working environment – friendly, flexible and supportive working environment which is built around great team support and rewarding opportunities

A dedication to your wellbeing through our peer support program, corporate health insurance discounts and wellness programs

Staff benefits including additional purchased leave, volunteer leave days, retail discounts and more!


How to Apply

 Apply now to join our team and help us shape the future of insurance and risk management through data-driven insights and cutting-edge technology.

Click on Apply for this Job to submit your application.

To be considered for this opportunity, you must have the right to live and work in Australia at the time of application. Please note that agency applications will not be considered for this position.

Shaping Spaces. Shaping Careers.
SHAPE Sydney is our largest team and national head office, with more than 180 professionals delivering construction excellence across one of Australia’s most dynamic markets. Backed by more than 36 years of experience as an ASX‑listed national business, we’re known for our reliable delivery, strong client relationships and consistent outcomes across projects ranging from $100k to $100m.
Within our Business Systems & Analytics team, we’re investing in world‑leading systems, data platforms and reporting capability to help the business make smarter decisions. This role sits at the centre of a national construction business, partnering with stakeholders across the organisation to turn complex data into clear, practical insights that support better outcomes for our projects, our people and our clients.

About the Role
Joining our Sydney team, the BI Analyst partners with stakeholders across the business to understand reporting needs, translate them into effective data solutions and deliver insights that support decision‑making. Working within our Business Systems & Analytics team, you’ll design dashboards, analyse data and help teams turn complex information into practical business outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Engage stakeholders to gather reporting requirements and translate them into BI solutions
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports using platforms such as Power BI
Analyse data to identify trends, insights and opportunities for improvement
Extract, transform and model data from multiple systems to support reliable reporting
Ensure reporting aligns with data governance, security and quality standards
Facilitate stakeholder workshops and review sessions to refine reporting needs
About You
You’re an analytically minded professional who enjoys partnering with stakeholders across the business to understand challenges and deliver meaningful insights.
Using your BI expertise and strong communication skills, you translate business needs into practical reporting solutions and help teams make informed decisions through clear, actionable data.

Key Requirements

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, analytics, business or similar
3-5 years’ experience in a BI or analytics role
Advanced Power BI and DAX capability
Strong SQL skills and experience with relational databases
Experience with data modelling and ETL processes
Strong stakeholder communication and influencing skills
Collaborative and solutions‑focused with a passion for data‑driven decisions
Why SHAPE is a great place to work
We have the ambitious vision to be ‘the place where everyone wants to work’. That’s why at SHAPE you’ll enjoy:

A diverse and inclusive culture, recognised by Human Synergistics and Australian Financial Review
5 additional paid leave days every year
Personalised career development to fast-track your growth with regular check-ins
An ‘Individual Flexibility Plan’ to match your work/life needs
Competitive salary and profit-share potential (up to 15% of your salary)
Health and wellbeing benefits, including mental health support, fitness activities, annual flu vaccinations, and discounted health insurance
Personalised parental leave benefits for your growing family
Regular social events to connect with your colleagues, subcontractors, and clients
